strategies.summary: The extremely low cardinality and near-constant values observed in multiple bulk-related backward and forward features (e23-e32) may indicate a structural uniformity or load concentration effect in the dataset, potentially reflecting a stable traffic pattern or saturation in these metrics.
strategies.strategic_objective: Clarify whether near-constant bulk feature values reflect true network saturation or data artifacts and assess their impact on subtle attack-related variations.

strategies.summary: The presence of perfect or near-perfect correlations (correlation=1 or 0.9993) among many feature pairs (e13-e22) suggests strong linear dependencies that may introduce redundancy, potentially reducing the effective dimensionality of the dataset.
strategies.strategic_objective: Determine stability and class-dependence of strong feature correlations and evaluate their effect on independent signal representation.

strategies.summary: The partition exhibits a moderate class imbalance ratio (1.577) and the Destination Port feature shows strong distributional divergence between BENIGN and DDoS classes, including high Jensen-Shannon divergence and entropy differences (e2, e33), suggesting that Destination Port may be a key discriminative feature.
strategies.strategic_objective: Investigate the nature and temporal stability of the zero entropy and distributional divergence in Destination Port between classes to understand its discriminative role.
